{{Otheruses|Jojo}}
{{Quote|Jojo...|Esege Tuketu mourning Jojo during the battle of Doornik-319|<ref name="SOL">''[[Shield of Lies]]''</ref>}}
'''Jojo''' was a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] with the [[24th Bombardment Squadron]], and used the designation [[Red Four/Legends|Red Four]] during the [[Battle of Doornik-319]].<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

==Biography==
Jojo's [[BTL-S8 K-wing assault starfighter|K-wing]] was disabled by an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] shot, forcing their weapons officer to disgorge the ship's complement of [[T-33 plasma torpedo]]es. With the fighter nonetheless out of control, Jojo could not pull out of the forced dive, and the K-wing crashed into a [[Yevetha]]n [[Aramadia-class thrustship|thrustship]]. Jojo was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the crash. Wingmate [[Esege Tuketu]], who flew Red Leader in the battle, was stricken by the loss of Jojo, and was overcome when the fellow pilot's name was posted on the list of casualties aboard their carrier ship.<ref name="SOL">''[[Shield of Lies]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Jojo and Red Four's weapons tech were unable to act when their fighter was disabled at Doornik-319, and thus was killed when it impacted into a Yevethan vessel. Jojo was popular with other pilots, and their friend Esege Tuketu was devastated when Jojo died.<ref name="SOL" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jojo is mentioned during a battle sequence in the novel ''[[Shield of Lies]]'' by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]]. No information on Jojo's species or gender is given.<ref name="SOL" />

==Appearances==
